acceptance_criteria.text: Warm-turn proxy tool-state mismatches must not surface as raw runtime failures; runtime must recover deterministically or fail with a controlled fallback.
proof: Historical prod snapshot shows raw call_id mismatch handler failures before the fix.
executor.ts now gates cold recovery for both thrown errors and response.error payload messages.
warm-cold-path.test.ts covers mismatch detection and one-shot recovery gating.
gaps: No fresh post-deploy organic prod mismatch was observed.

acceptance_criteria.text: Private ESI token decrypt/auth failures must degrade cleanly without crashing Telegram turns.
proof: sso.ts now catches decryptStoredSecret failures and returns null with actionable secret-safe logging.
handlers.ts maps auth-state/decrypt failures to a relink-required user message.
sso.test.ts covers malformed encrypted token storage and passes.
gaps: No live prod token was intentionally corrupted for verification.

acceptance_criteria.text: Moon-like requests must have a bounded observable execution path and include production latency evidence.
proof: Historical prod logs show the old path took 8 iterations with web_search drift and 117389 input tokens.
tools.ts adds deterministic count_moons over local mapPlanets data and prompts.ts routes current-region moon questions into it.
Post-fix prod reproduction finishes in 2 iterations with TOOL_COUNT=1 and TOOLS=count_moons.
Direct proxy probe shows minimal /responses latency of about 1.34s.
gaps: Post-fix moon reproduction still took about 28.5s overall due to model round-trips and prompt size.

acceptance_criteria.text: Adjacent recurrence paths must be hardened or documented precisely.
proof: getAccessToken hardening covers all private ESI call paths.
tool-state recovery hardening covers all warm continuations that submit function_call_output items.
SDE schema text now exposes mapPlanets/sde_raw_records to reduce recurrence for nearby static celestial count questions.
docs/RELIABILITY.md documents the new runtime guarantees and residual risk.
gaps: Telegram polling conflicts remain out of scope.

acceptance_criteria.text: Evidence must distinguish proxy/API behavior from app/runtime behavior on production.
proof: prod-pm2-log-snapshot.txt captures app/runtime failures and historical prod request traces.
prod-deploy.txt proves the deployed commit and PM2 online state.
Local raw artifacts cover test, typecheck, lint, and build.

commands_for_fresh_verifier: npm run test -- tests/unit/warm-cold-path.test.ts tests/unit/moon-count.test.ts tests/unit/sso.test.ts tests/unit/tools.test.ts
known_gaps: No fresh organic Telegram user message after deploy was captured in PM2 logs.
Moon requests are materially faster in tool usage but still pay for two Responses round-trips.
